title: Human Resources Cum Admin Deputy Manager
description:
About our client: Our client is an EPC company specializing in the chemical, petrochemical, and high-tech industries.

About the role: 

  • Support the full spectrum of Human Resource functions, including recruitment and selection, employee relations, payroll management, and training and development.
  • Responsible for full payroll administration.
  • Execute HR operational functions related to employee data maintenance, leave administration, employee benefits, medical claims, and work pass applications.
  • Assist in reviewing HR policies and process flows.
  • Support the recruitment process, including sourcing, shortlisting, and onboarding of employees.
  • Support the consolidation of HR reports and analysis for the Group, and generate statistical reports as required.
  • Develop internal work plans to ensure deliverables and timelines are met.
  • Provide timely reporting to the Finance department.
  • Ensure organizational compliance with statutory Act requirements.
  • Communicate and educate staff on regulatory requirements to drive ongoing awareness.
  • Promote workplace safety and health awareness, and ensure staff are informed of relevant statutory requirements.
  • Manage and supervise the Office Administration function.
  • Oversee overall Office and HR Administration matters, including office lease management, workspace allocation, wellness programs, season parking, and staff passes.
  • Manage the budget for Office and HR Administration.

About you:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Studies, or Business Administration.
  • At least five years of relevant experience, specifically in HR governance, people risk management, HR budgeting, and office administration, including 5–8 years in a people management role.
  • Well versed in HRIS systems such as JustLogin and Unit4.
  • Experience in rolling out HRIS implementation projects within an organization.
  • Good knowledge of employment laws and regulations.
  • Strong communicator with excellent interpersonal and influencing skills.
  • Resourceful, independent, meticulous, with a strong desire to learn and able to work in an agile environment.
  • Able to work independently with integrity under minimal supervision, as well as collaboratively across teams.
  • Available to commence work immediately.


Other info:

  • Work location: Jurong East
  • Work hours: Monday - Friday (9am – 6pm)

*Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.*


Employment Agency: Kingsforce Management Services Pte. Ltd.

Employment Agency Licence No: 94C3545

EA Personnel: Kimberly Wong

EA Personnel Reg. No.: R1984508
